learn.heading: Learn
learn.subtitle: How a pure Nostr client works, what NIP-29 groups are, and how to bring your own relay.
learn.card.whatIsObelisk.title: What is Obelisk?
learn.card.whatIsObelisk.desc: A Discord-style group chat with no backend. Just your Nostr keys and the relays you choose.
learn.card.howObeliskWorks.title: How it works
learn.card.howObeliskWorks.desc: Frontend-only, signed events, NIP-29 groups. No database, no servers, no accounts.
learn.card.webOfTrust.title: Bring your own relay
learn.card.webOfTrust.desc: You pick the relays. Switch them, add more, run your own. The app stays the same.
learn.card.futureNostrRelays.title: What's on the roadmap
learn.card.futureNostrRelays.desc: Voice channels, NIP-46 bunker login, NIP-44 encryption, and a static-export build.
learn.card.bitcoinZaps.title: Bitcoin zaps in chat
learn.card.bitcoinZaps.desc: Send sats from any message via NIP-57 zaps and Nostr Wallet Connect. No platform, no fee.
learn.card.adminCli.title: NIP-29 admins & roles
learn.card.adminCli.desc: Group moderation lives on the relay. Admins, members, kicks and bans are standard NIP-29 events.
faq.heading: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Short answers about the new Nostr-only Obelisk.
faq.q1.question: What is Obelisk?
faq.q1.answer: Obelisk is an open-source, Discord-style group chat client that runs entirely on Nostr. There is no backend and no database — every message is a signed Nostr event published directly to the relays you choose. Groups are NIP-29 groups, DMs are encrypted Nostr DMs, and your account is a Nostr keypair.
faq.q2.question: Is there a server I have to sign up to?
faq.q2.answer: No. Obelisk is a frontend. There is no signup, no email, no password, no Obelisk account. You log in with a Nostr key and the app talks straight to the relays you've configured. We don't run an Obelisk server, we don't have a database, and we never see your messages.
faq.q3.question: What relays does Obelisk use?
faq.q3.answer: Whatever relays you tell it to. The default for groups is wss://relay.obelisk.ar, but you can add, remove, or replace any relay from the relay rail in the app. Each relay you add appears as its own 'server' icon on the left, exactly like Discord guilds — except the server is the relay, not us.
faq.q4.question: Do I need an email or phone number?
faq.q4.answer: No. Obelisk has no signup form. You log in with a Nostr key — via a browser extension (NIP-07) or by pasting an nsec. NIP-46 bunker support is on the roadmap.
faq.q5.question: How are groups implemented?
faq.q5.answer: Obelisk uses NIP-29: relay-managed groups. Channels, members, admins, kicks and bans are all standard NIP-29 events that the relay enforces. Any NIP-29-compatible relay works. You can host your own relay, run a private one for your community, or use a public one.
faq.q6.question: Are my messages encrypted?
faq.q6.answer: Direct messages are end-to-end encrypted using Nostr DMs (NIP-04 today, NIP-17 gift-wrapped supported, NIP-44 v2 on the roadmap). Group messages are signed but public on the relay — anyone the relay lets in can read them, just like a Discord channel. Run a private relay if you need a closed group.
faq.q7.question: Can I self-host Obelisk?
faq.q7.answer: Yes — Obelisk is a Next.js app and 'hosting' it just means serving static-style HTML/JS. There's no database to manage and no backend service to run. Run it locally with `npm run dev`, ship it to any Node host, or expose it through a tunnel. We're working on a static-export build for one-click deployment to GitHub Pages, S3, or any static CDN.
faq.q8.question: How do I get a Nostr key?
faq.q8.answer: You can generate one in the login screen. If you want a standalone signer, install a NIP-07 browser extension like Alby or nos2x, or on mobile use Amber (Android) or nsec.app. Keep your nsec safe — it's your account, no one can recover it for you.
faq.q9.question: What about voice channels?
faq.q9.answer: Voice is on the roadmap. The previous voice implementation used a Socket.io signaling server, which doesn't fit a backend-free architecture — we're rebuilding it as P2P WebRTC with relay-based signaling so it stays pure Nostr.
faq.q10.question: Does Obelisk work on mobile?
faq.q10.answer: Yes. Obelisk is a responsive web app and works in any modern mobile browser. Pair it with a NIP-07-compatible signer (or a NIP-46 bunker, once shipped) for a smooth login. A dedicated PWA build is on the roadmap.
hero.title: Tus comunidades,
hero.subtitle: Discord-style group chat built directly on Nostr relays.
hero.trustLine: No accounts. No backend. Your keys, your relays.
features.heading: Why Obelisk
features.subtitle: All the chat. None of the servers. Your keys, your relays, signed events.
features.nostrIdentity.title: Nostr login — your keys, your account
features.nostrIdentity.desc: Sign in with NIP-07 or your nsec. There is no Obelisk account to create. Your Nostr key is the account, everywhere.
features.realtimeChat.title: NIP-29 groups
features.realtimeChat.desc: Channels, members, admins, kicks and bans are all standard NIP-29 events on the relay. Open protocol, no proprietary format, no lock-in.
features.encryptedDMs.title: Encrypted DMs
features.encryptedDMs.desc: Direct messages are end-to-end encrypted Nostr DMs (NIP-04 + NIP-17 gift-wrapped). Only you and the recipient can read them.
features.voice.title: P2P voice channels over Nostr
features.voice.desc: Talk to anyone in a channel with no media server in the middle. Voice is WebRTC peer-to-peer; signaling rides on Nostr events, just like every other message.
features.selfHosted.title: No backend, no database
features.selfHosted.desc: Obelisk is a frontend. There is no Postgres, no Socket.io server, no auth service. Every message is a signed event sent straight to a relay.
features.spamResistant.title: Static-site self-hosting
features.spamResistant.desc: Self-hosting means serving HTML and JS. No database to operate, no Docker required. Run it from your laptop, a tunnel, or a static host.
features.zaps.title: Bitcoin zaps in chat
features.zaps.desc: Send sats from any message via NIP-57 zaps and Nostr Wallet Connect (NIP-47). No custodian, no platform fee, no middleman.
features.adminCli.title: Open source
features.adminCli.desc: MIT-licensed, every line on GitHub. Read it, fork it, audit it, run it. The whole app is the client you can see.
steps.heading: Three steps
steps.subtitle: No signup forms. No email verification. Just keys and relays.
steps.1.title: Sign in with Nostr
steps.1.desc: Use a NIP-07 extension or paste an nsec. No signup forms, no email, no password.
steps.2.title: Pick a relay
steps.2.desc: The default groups relay is preloaded. Add any NIP-29 relay you like — each one becomes its own 'server' in the rail.
steps.3.title: Chat
steps.3.desc: Join NIP-29 groups, post messages, react, reply, DM. Every action is a signed Nostr event going straight to the relay.
roadmap.heading: Roadmap
roadmap.subtitle: Building in the open. From Nostr-only client today to a richer, more portable client tomorrow.
roadmap.phase0.title: Identity
roadmap.phase1.title: NIP-29 Groups
roadmap.phase1.items: Group create / edit metadata|Channels, replies, reactions|Admins, members, kicks, bans (relay-enforced)
roadmap.phase1_5.title: Multi-relay UX
roadmap.phase1_5.items: Add / remove relays from the rail|Switch active relay per group|Per-relay favicon and metadata
roadmap.phase2.title: Encrypted DMs
roadmap.phase2.items: NIP-04 encrypted DMs|NIP-17 gift-wrapped DMs|DM inbox via NIP-17 inbox relays
roadmap.phase3.title: Lightning Zaps
roadmap.phase3.items: NIP-57 zap requests|Nostr Wallet Connect (NIP-47)|Per-message zap amounts
roadmap.phase6.title: Backend removal
roadmap.phase6.items: Drop PostgreSQL and Prisma|Drop Socket.io server|Frontend talks directly to relays
roadmap.phase4.title: Auth & encryption upgrades
roadmap.phase4.items: NIP-46 bunker (remote signer)|NIP-44 v2 encryption migration|Static-export build (no Node required)
roadmap.phase5.title: Voice & PWA
roadmap.phase5.items: P2P voice channels with relay signaling|Installable PWA with push notifications|Offline-first message cache
stack.heading: Tech stack
stack.subtitle: Modern, open-source, no proprietary backend.
cta.heading: Ready to chat without a server
cta.subtitle: Open the app, log in with your Nostr keys, pick a relay. That's it.
footer.brandBlurb: Discord-style group chat with no backend. Your keys, your relays, signed Nostr events.
profile.title: Profile
profile.nicknamePlaceholder: Leave empty to use your Nostr name
profileEditor.setupDesc: Add a name and picture so others can recognize you.
profileEditor.nameRequiredHint: You need a name to continue. Tap the dice to generate one.
profileEditor.clickToUpload: Click to upload or drag & drop an image
profileEditor.invalidUrl: Could not load image from this URL
profileEditor.publishError: Failed to publish profile. Try again.
profileEditor.confirmDesc: This will publish your updated profile (kind 0) to Nostr relays. Your other profile fields (NIP-05, Lightning, etc.) will be preserved.
inbox.title: Inbox
landing.preview.heading: See it in action
landing.preview.subtitle: The same Nostr-only client, designed for both your laptop and your phone. Take the tour for the device you actually use.
landing.preview.desktop.title: Discord-style chat, on the open Nostr stack
landing.preview.desktop.desc: Server rail, categorized channels, threaded forums, member list, voice with SFU. The full workspace in your browser.
landing.preview.desktop.alt: Obelisk desktop screenshot — General Chat with server rail, channel list, message stream and member list.
landing.preview.mobile.title: Mobile-first server and channel list
landing.preview.mobile.desc: Tap your way through servers, categories, voice and forums. Install it as a PWA — no app store required.
landing.preview.mobile.alt: Obelisk mobile screenshot — server icons, General Chat, nested forum channels, voice channel and uncategorized list.
landing.showcase.heading: One client, every screen
landing.showcase.subtitle: Real-time voice rooms with video, channels in your pocket — same Nostr-only client, no native app, no backend.
landing.showcase.desktop.alt: Obelisk desktop screenshot — voice channel with an SFU peer transmitting a colour test pattern.
landing.showcase.mobile.alt: Obelisk mobile screenshot — server list with categorised channels and a live voice room.
mobile.hero.title: Group chat that fits in your
mobile.hero.subtitle: Obelisk on Android, iOS and any modern mobile browser. NIP-29 channels, voice with SFU, encrypted DMs and Lightning zaps — all backed by your Nostr keys, no email, no password.
mobile.shot1.title: Discord-style server list, mobile-first
mobile.shot1.alt: Obelisk mobile home — server icons up top, then categorized channels: General Chat, Forum (Finances, Bitcoin, RUNSTR), Voice Channel, Feedback, Uncategorized.
mobile.shot1.desc: Your relays show up as servers. Inside each, channels are grouped into the categories the operator publishes — general chat, forums, voice, anything you want — with unread badges and live indicators just like Discord.
mobile.shot1.features: Pinned server rail at the top|Operator-controlled categories|Forum threads nested under their parent|Live indicators on active voice channels
mobile.shot2.title: Voice and video that scales past mesh
mobile.shot2.alt: Obelisk mobile voice channel running in SFU mode with a test peer transmitting an SMPTE color-bar video stream.
mobile.shot2.desc: Small rooms run as a P2P mesh; once a relay advertises an SFU, the same channel switches automatically and rooms scale to dozens of participants — all signaled over Nostr (kind 25050) without revealing identity to a media server.
mobile.shot2.features: Mesh up to ~6 peers, SFU beyond|Mute, deafen, screen-share and chat from one bar|Bottom nav stays put while you talk
mobile.shot3.title: No email, no password, three ways to log in
mobile.shot3.alt: Obelisk mobile login modal showing three options — NIP-46 remote signer, generate a new key, and import an existing nsec.
mobile.shot3.desc: Pick the signer that matches your habits — a remote bunker via NIP-46 (Amber, nsec.app), generate a brand-new key on device, or paste an nsec you already own. Onboarding is one screen.
mobile.shot3.features: NIP-46 bunker via QR or URL|Generate-and-back-up flow for new users|Paste your existing nsec — your keys, your account
mobile.shot4.title: Your Nostr profile, your settings
mobile.shot4.alt: Obelisk mobile profile screen — banner, avatar, npub and hex pubkey, Edit Nostr Profile button, Disconnect button and a Preferences tab.
mobile.shot4.desc: The same kind 0 profile every Nostr client uses — display name, banner, bio, NIP-05, Lightning address. Edit it once and every Nostr app sees the change.
mobile.shot4.features: Copy npub or hex pubkey with one tap|Open your profile in any other Nostr client|Per-device preferences kept in localStorage
mobile.cta.heading: Take Obelisk with you
mobile.cta.subtitle: Open the web app on your phone, install it from the browser menu, and you're done. No app store, no review, no platform tax.
desktop.hero.title: A Discord-class workspace, on the open
desktop.hero.subtitle: Obelisk in your browser: server rail, categorized channels, threaded forums, member list, mediasoup-powered voice rooms, encrypted DMs and Lightning zaps — all on relays you control.
desktop.shot1.title: Three-pane chat, member list and reactions
desktop.shot1.alt: Obelisk desktop view of the public General Chat — server rail, channel list, central message stream with reactions and a link preview, and the right-side member list.
desktop.shot1.desc: The familiar Discord layout — server rail, channel list, message stream, member sidebar — wired straight to NIP-29 events on the relay. Every message is a signed Nostr event: no backend, no proprietary format.
desktop.shot1.features: Replies, reactions and rich link previews|Member list reflects the relay's NIP-29 roster live|Lightning zap any message via NIP-57 + NWC
desktop.shot2.title: Threaded forums for slower, deeper conversations
desktop.shot2.alt: Obelisk desktop forum view of plaza-publica — four threads (Politica y Economia, But why?, Only-Claws, Finanzas-Trading v2) with OP, message count and last reply time.
desktop.shot2.desc: Forum-kind channels turn into Discord-style threaded boards: a tidy list of topics, OP and last-reply metadata, and the same NIP-29 moderation as every other channel.
desktop.shot2.features: One-click 'New thread' to start a topic|Last-message timestamps stay accurate via cursors|Same admin model — kicks and bans live on the relay
desktop.shot3.title: Big voice rooms with mediasoup and screen share
desktop.shot3.alt: Obelisk desktop voice channel in SFU mode showing a test peer streaming an SMPTE color-bar test pattern with the chat sidebar and voice controls.
desktop.shot3.desc: Voice channels start as a P2P mesh and switch to a mediasoup SFU when the relay advertises one — same UI, same kind-25050 signaling, just more peers and screen-share without melting CPUs.
desktop.shot3.features: Allow-listed SFU operators per relay|Screen share with hardware-accelerated encode|All signaling rides on Nostr — no sticky-session backend
desktop.cta.heading: Open Obelisk in any browser
desktop.cta.subtitle: It's a static frontend with no signup. Open it locally, install the PWA, or self-host the build — same client either way.
preferences.activity.label: Show activity indicator
preferences.activity.description: Bottom-right notifications for login, signing, and relay publishing.
preferences.localData.title: Local data
preferences.localData.clear.title: Clear local cache
preferences.localData.clear.description: Wipes cached channels, profiles, branding, member lists, read positions, and DM cache. Live data re-streams from the relay on next paint. Your session and preferences are kept.
preferences.localData.confirm.title: Clear local cache?
preferences.localData.confirm.descriptio: This wipes every locally-cached relay response (channels, profiles, branding, members, read positions, DM cache, UI flags) and reloads the page. Your session and preferences stay; you stay logged in.
preferences.language.label: Language
preferences.language.description: Choose the language used by the app UI.
